name: Small ball
description: A strategic style of play that sacrifices height, physical strength, and traditional post presence in favor of speed, agility, and elite perimeter shooting. A small-ball lineup typically replaces a traditional center or power forward with smaller, faster wings or guards. The goal is to maximize court spacing (often using a "5-out" alignment), push the pace in transition, create defensive mismatches, and generate high-value three-point opportunities.
